briefdescription.para: UI control rendering mode (default mode).

detaileddescription.para: This mode is designed for UI controls that can overlap. In this mode renderer order will be respective to the tree hierarchy of Actors.

The rendering order is depth first, so for the following actor tree, A will be drawn first, then B, D, E, then C, F. This ensures that overlapping actors are drawn as expected (whereas, with breadth first traversal, the actors would interleave).
To change the order of sibling actors, use the Actor::Raise and Actor::Lower APIs. Within an actor, the Renderer depth index dictates the order the renderers are drawn.

briefdescription.para: Layer will use depth test.

detaileddescription.para: This mode is designed for a 3 dimensional scene where actors in front of other actors will obscure them, i.e. the actors are sorted by the distance from the camera.

When using this mode, a depth test will be used. A depth clear will happen for each layer, which means actors in a layer "above" other layers will be rendered in front of actors in those layers regardless of their Z positions (see Layer::Raise() and Layer::Lower()).

Opaque renderers are drawn first and write to the depth buffer. Then transparent renderers are drawn with depth test enabled but depth write switched off. Transparent renderers are drawn based on their distance from the camera. A renderer's DEPTH_INDEX property is used to offset the distance to the camera when ordering transparent renderers.

This is useful if you want to define the draw order of two or more transparent renderers that are equal distance from the camera. Unlike LAYER_UI, parent-child relationship does not affect rendering order at all.
@SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Enumeration for the behavior of the layer.

detaileddescription.para: Check each value to see how it affects the layer. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: The sort function type.
Creates an empty Layer handle.

detaileddescription.para: This can be initialized with Layer::New(...). @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Copy constructor.
Assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
The actor to copy
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Move constructor.
Move assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.22
rhs
The layer to move
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Increments the depth of the layer.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
Layer is on the stage.

briefdescription.para: Decrements the depth of the layer.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
Layer is on the stage.

briefdescription.para: Ensures the layers depth is greater than the target layer.

detaileddescription.para:
If the layer already is above the target layer, its depth is not changed. If the layer was below target, its new depth will be immediately above target. @SINCE_1_0.0
target
Layer to get above of
Layer is on the stage.
Target layer is on the stage.
All layers between this layer and target get new depth values.

briefdescription.para: Ensures the layers depth is less than the target layer.

detaileddescription.para:
If the layer already is below the target layer, its depth is not changed. If the layer was above target, its new depth will be immediately below target. @SINCE_1_0.0
target
Layer to get below of
Layer is on the stage.
Target layer is on the stage.
All layers between this layer and target get new depth values.

briefdescription.para: Raises the layer to the top. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Lowers the layer to the bottom. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Moves the layer directly above the given layer.

detaileddescription.para:
After the call, this layers depth will be immediately above target. @SINCE_1_0.0
target
Layer to get on top of
Layer is on the stage.
Target layer is on the stage.
All layers between this layer and target get new depth values.

briefdescription.para: Moves the layer directly below the given layer.

detaileddescription.para:
After the call, this layers depth will be immediately below target. @SINCE_1_0.0
target
Layer to get below of
Layer is on the stage.
Target layer is on the stage.
All layers between this layer and target get new depth values.

briefdescription.para: This allows the user to specify the sort function that the layer should use.

detaileddescription.para: The sort function is used to determine the order in which the actors are drawn and input is processed on the actors in the layer.
A function of the following type should be used: floatYourSortFunction(constVector3&position);

@SINCE_1_0.0
function
The sort function pointer
If the sort function returns a low number, the actor with the data will be drawn in front of an actor whose data yields a high value from the sort function.
All child layers use the same sort function. If a child layer is added to this layer, then the sort function used by the child layer will also be the same.

briefdescription.para: Creates a Layer object.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
A handle to a newly allocated Layer

briefdescription.para: Downcasts a handle to Layer handle.

detaileddescription.para:
If handle points to a Layer, the downcast produces valid handle. If not, the returned handle is left uninitialized. @SINCE_1_0.0
handle
Handle to an object
Handle to a Layer or an uninitialized handle

briefdescription.para: Layers provide a mechanism for overlaying groups of actors on top of each other.

detaileddescription.para: When added to a scene, a layer can be ordered relative to other layers. The bottom layer is at depth zero. A scene provides a default layer for its children.

Layered actors inherit position etc. as normal, but are drawn in an order determined by the layers. In case of LAYER_3D, the depth buffer is cleared before each layer is rendered unless depth test is disabled or there's no need for it based on the layer's contents; actors in lower layers cannot obscure actors in higher layers.

A layer has either LAYER_UI or LAYER_3D mode. LAYER_UI has better performance, the depth test is disabled, and a child actor hides its parent actor. LAYER_3D uses the depth test, thus a close actor hides a farther one. LAYER_UI is the default mode and recommended for general cases. See Layer::Behavior and SetBehavior() for more information.

Layer is a type of Actor, thus can have parent or children actors. A layer influences rendering of its all descendant actors, until another layer appears in the actor tree and manages its own subtree.
If depth test is disabled, there is no performance overhead from clearing the depth buffer.

Actions
Action Name
Layer method called
raise
Raise()
lower
Lower()
raiseToTop
RaiseToTop()
lowerToBottom
LowerToBottom()
@SINCE_1_0.0
